question: In modification mode, the operator wants to cancel every running token on the `Send notification` service task in a single staged modification. Which exact action does this?

options.text: Apply the modifications first, then remove each token individually from the instance history view.
Select `Send notification` in the diagram, then click `Cancel instance` from the dropdown.
Click `Apply Modifications` in the footer and choose a `Cancel All Tokens` shortcut from the modal.
Right-click each running token in the instance history and choose `Terminate token`.

a.text: Cancel modifications are staged before being applied; applying first and then removing tokens is not the documented workflow.

b.text: The docs prescribe selecting the flow node and clicking `Cancel instance` from the dropdown to cancel all running tokens on that flow node as a pending modification.

c.text: `Apply Modifications` opens the summary modal but offers no `Cancel All Tokens` shortcut there.

d.text: Operate has no documented `Terminate token` right-click action in the instance history.

explanation: Selecting the flow node in the diagram and choosing `Cancel instance` from the dropdown stages a single pending modification that cancels every running token on that node, which is then visible in the instance history until the operator applies the batch.
